AuthorizationStatusEnumType.description: Current status of the ID Token.

AuthorizeCertificateStatusEnumType.description: Certificate status information. - if all certificates are valid: return 'Accepted'. - if one of the certificates was revoked, return 'CertificateRevoked'.

CustomDataType.description: This class does not get 'AdditionalProperties = false' in the schema generation, so it can be extended with arbitrary JSON properties to allow adding custom data.

AdditionalInfoType.description: Contains a case insensitive identifier to use for the authorization and the type of authorization to support multiple forms of identifiers.

additionalIdToken.description: This field specifies the additional IdToken.

type.description: This defines the type of the additionalIdToken. This is a custom type, so the implementation needs to be agreed upon by all involved parties.

IdTokenType.description: Contains a case insensitive identifier to use for the authorization and the type of authorization to support multiple forms of identifiers.

idToken.description: IdToken is case insensitive. Might hold the hidden id of an RFID tag, but can for example also contain a UUID.

MessageContentType.description: Message details, for a message to be displayed on a Charging Station.

language.description: Message language identifier. Contains a language code as defined in [RFC5646].

IdTokenInfoType.description: ID_ Token. Status_ Info. Authorization status information.

cacheExpiryDateTime.description: Date and Time after which the token must be considered invalid.

chargingPriority.description: Priority from a business point of view. Default priority is 0, The range is from -9 to 9. Higher values indicate a higher priority.

language1.description: Preferred user interface language of identifier user. Contains a language code as defined in [RFC5646].

evseId.description: Only used when the IdToken is only valid for one or more specific EVSEs, not for the entire Charging Station.

language2.description: Second preferred user interface language of identifier user. Don't use when language1 is omitted, has to be different from language1.
